title: Things to learn — July 2023 curiosity list
html:
Things to learn — July 2023 curiosity list

Source: 2023-07-10 Apple Note. Seven-item learning queue captured pre-Jordan-trip. A direct early signal of the AI as fate thesis percolating during junior summer.

TL;DR

  • AI-cluster items: principle information theory, synthetic training data, AI verification, [[effective-accelerationism|effect[ive] accelerationism]] — four of seven items are AI/ML-adjacent, confirming AI-as-fate was already the dominant pull six months before the Dec 2023 "build something" turn.
  • great man theory — first explicit mention; ladders into the career archetypes spy leader creator reading later.
  • "What happened to sriracha" — pop-supply-chain curiosity (Huy Fong shortage era), the only non-AI item; same mind that later asks why-this-product-fails questions.
  • "What does intellectual honesty mean" — meta-epistemic question, a recurring concern that surfaces again in the 2024 07 04 questions mega notebook.
  • Whole list reads as the inbound queue for what he didn't yet know he was building toward.

Pattern

This is the earliest pure-curiosity note in 2023 — no project framing, no person, no plan. Just "here are the things I want to understand." The AI weighting shows where attention was actually going before he had language for it.

Open questions

  • Did he ever actually research any of these, or is this write-once-and-forget?
  • Where did "effect accelerationism" land for him? (e/acc was peaking on Twitter mid-2023.)
